# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].

Prevent SQL injection.
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
module Admin
  class ReportsController < ApplicationController
    before_action :authenticate_admin!
    before_action :authorize_reporting_access!

    # Maximum rows returned to prevent resource exhaustion
    MAX_ROWS = 10_000
    QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30

    # Allowlisted tables that can be queried
    ALLOWED_TABLES = %w[
      orders products users invoices payments
      page_views sessions analytics_events
    ].freeze

    # Completely forbidden SQL keywords/patterns
    FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS = [
      /\bINSERT\b/i,
      /\bUPDATE\b/i,
      /\bDELETE\b/i,
      /\bDROP\b/i,
      /\bALTER\b/i,
      /\bCREATE\b/i,
      /\bTRUNCATE\b/i,
      /\bGRANT\b/i,
      /\bREVOKE\b/i,
      /\bEXEC\b/i,
      /\bEXECUTE\b/i,
      /\bINTO\s+OUTFILE\b/i,
      /\bINTO\s+DUMPFILE\b/i,
      /\bLOAD_FILE\b/i,
      /\bLOAD\s+DATA\b/i,
      /\bINFORMATION_SCHEMA\b/i,
      /\bpg_/i,
      /\bmysql\./i,
      /\bSLEEP\s*\(/i,
      /\bBENCHMARK\s*\(/i,
      /\bWAITFOR\b/i,
      /\bpg_sleep\b/i,
      /;\s*\S/,  # multiple statements (statement stacking)
      /--/,      # SQL comments
      /\/\*/,    # block comments
      /\bUNION\b.*\bSELECT\b.*\bFROM\b.*\b(pg_|information_schema|mysql\.)/i,
    ].freeze

    def new
      @saved_reports = SavedReport.where(user: current_admin)
    end

    def create
      raw_query = params[:query].to_s.strip

      validation_error = validate_query(raw_query)
      if validation_error
        render json: { error: validation_error }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        return
      end

      # Wrap in a read-only transaction with timeout and row limit
      results = execute_safe_query(raw_query)

      log_query_execution(raw_query, results[:row_count])

      render json: {
        columns: results[:columns],
        rows: results[:rows],
        row_count: results[:row_count],
        truncated: results[:truncated],
        executed_at: Time.current.iso8601
      }
    rescue QueryValidationError => e
      render json: { error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
      # Sanitize error message to avoid leaking schema info
      sanitized_message = sanitize_error_message(e.message)
      render json: { error: "Query execution failed: #{sanitized_message}" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    rescue QueryTimeoutError
      render json: { error: "Query exceeded maximum execution time of #{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS} seconds." }, status: :request_timeout
    end

    private

    class QueryValidationError < StandardError; end
    class QueryTimeoutError < StandardError; end

    def validate_query(query)
      return "Query cannot be blank." if query.blank?
      return "Query is too long (max 5000 characters)." if query.length > 5000
      return "Query must be a SELECT statement." unless query.match?(/\A\s*SELECT\b/i)

      # Check for forbidden patterns
      FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS.each do |pattern|
        if query.match?(pattern)
          return "Query contains forbidden syntax: #{pattern.source.gsub(/\\b/, '').gsub(/\\s\*/, ' ')[0..30]}"
        end
      end

      # Check for multiple statements (semicolons not at the very end)
      stripped = query.gsub(/;\s*\z/, '')
      return "Multiple SQL statements are not allowed." if stripped.include?(';')

      # Validate that only allowed tables are referenced
      table_validation_error = validate_tables(query)
      return table_validation_error if table_validation_error

      nil
    end

    def validate_tables(query)
      # Use a simple parser to extract table references
      # This extracts tokens after FROM and JOIN keywords
      table_references = extract_table_references(query)

      unauthorized_tables = table_references - ALLOWED_TABLES
      if unauthorized_tables.any?
        return "Access denied to table(s): #{unauthorized_tables.join(', ')}. Allowed tables: #{ALLOWED_TABLES.join(', ')}"
      end

      nil
    end

    def extract_table_references(query)
      tables = []

      # Match table names after FROM and JOIN (handles aliases)
      # FROM table_name [AS] alias
      # JOIN table_name [AS] alias
      query.scan(/\b(?:FROM|JOIN)\s+([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)/i) do |match|
        tables << match[0].downcase
      end

      tables.uniq
    end

    def execute_safe_query(query)
      columns = []
      rows = []
      truncated = false

      # Use a dedicated read-only connection approach
      # Wrap in a read-only transaction to prevent any writes
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection_pool.with_connection do |connection|
        connection.transaction do
          # Set the transaction to read-only (PostgreSQL)
          if connection.adapter_name.downcase.include?('postgresql')
            connection.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY")
            connection.execute("SET LOCAL statement_timeout = '#{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s'")
          elsif connection.adapter_name.downcase.include?('mysql')
            connection.execute("SET SESSION MAX_EXECUTION_TIME = #{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS * 1000}")
          end

          # Apply row limit by wrapping the query
          limited_query = apply_row_limit(query)

          result = connection.execute(limited_query)

          if result.respond_to?(:fields)
            columns = result.fields
          elsif result.respond_to?(:columns)
            columns = result.columns
          end

          result.each_with_index do |row, index|
            if index >= MAX_ROWS
              truncated = true
              break
            end

            # Convert row to array if it's a hash
            rows << (row.is_a?(Hash) ? row.values : row)
          end

          # Always rollback - we never want to commit anything from user queries
          raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
        end
      end

      { columns: columns, rows: rows, row_count: rows.size, truncated: truncated }
    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
      if e.message.include?('statement timeout') || e.message.include?('MAX_EXECUTION_TIME')
        raise QueryTimeoutError, "Query timed out"
      end
      raise
    end

    def apply_row_limit(query)
      # Remove any existing LIMIT to enforce our own
      # Then wrap with our limit
      clean_query = query.gsub(/;\s*\z/, '')

      if clean_query.match?(/\bLIMIT\s+\d+/i)
        # Replace existing LIMIT if it exceeds our max
        clean_query.gsub(/\bLIMIT\s+(\d+)/i) do
          user_limit = ::Regexp.last_match(1).to_i
          "LIMIT #{[user_limit, MAX_ROWS + 1].min}"
        end
      else
        "#{clean_query} LIMIT #{MAX_ROWS + 1}"
      end
    end

    def log_query_execution(query, row_count)
      Rails.logger.info(
        "[ADMIN_REPORT] user_id=#{current_admin.id} " \
        "email=#{current_admin.email} " \
        "query=#{query.truncate(500)} " \
        "row_count=#{row_count} " \
        "ip=#{request.remote_ip} " \
        "timestamp=#{Time.current.iso8601}"
      )

      # Also store in audit log table
      ReportAuditLog.create!(
        admin_user: current_admin,
        query_text: query,
        row_count: row_count,
        ip_address: request.remote_ip,
        user_agent: request.user_agent
      )
    end

    def sanitize_error_message(message)
      # Remove potentially sensitive info from error messages
      message
        .gsub(/PG::.*?:/, 'Database error:')
        .gsub(/Mysql2::Error:/, 'Database error:')
        .truncate(200)
    end

    def authenticate_admin!
      unless current_admin&.active?
        render json: { error: "Authentication required" }, status: :unauthorized
      end
    end

    def authorize_reporting_access!
      unless current_admin&.has_role?(:report_admin)
        render json: { error: "You do not have permission to run custom reports." }, status: :forbidden
      end
    end
  end
end
